footer.description: Embrace plant-based living. Every conscious choice builds a healthier planet and a more compassionate future.

hero.description: Discover the beauty of plant-based living. Every choice you make contributes to a healthier planet, stronger community, and compassionate future. Join thousands making the conscious switch to veganism.

veganForAnimalsPage.title: Animal Rights
veganForAnimalsPage.animalSuffering: It is known that animals feel pain and suffering and exhibit fear. We cannot fully understand their thought processes, but there is ample evidence that animals have their own societies and ways of life. Who has the right to declare that their rights should be any less than those that humans aspire to?
veganForAnimalsPage.birdsDebeaking: In the United States, almost all birds are raised on factory farms. In such stressful, crowded conditions, chickens peck each other severely. To stop this, workers cut off 2/3 of their beaks with hot knives, causing pain that lasts for weeks.
veganForAnimalsPage.broilerGrowth: Experts say broiler chickens grow so fast that their hearts and lungs can't support the rest of their bodies, resulting in many heart attacks and deaths.
veganForAnimalsPage.brokenNeckImg: A dairy cow with a broken neck left to die
veganForAnimalsPage.cowAndCalfImg: Cows and calves feel a strong bond
veganForAnimalsPage.description: Anyone who eats meat participates in animal slaughter through financial support. Animals feel pain, suffering, and fear.
veganForAnimalsPage.economicEfficiency: Many believe animals raised for food must be treated well because sick or dead ones wouldn't benefit agrobusiness. Dr. Bernard Rollin explains: 'Economically it's more efficient to put more birds in each cage, accepting lower productivity per bird for higher productivity per cage... Chickens are cheap, cages are expensive.'
veganForAnimalsPage.factoryFarms: Competition for cheap production of meat, eggs, and dairy has generated treatment of animals as objects or commodities in the agricultural business. The global trend is to replace family farms with factory farms: warehouses where animals are kept in overcrowded pens or narrow stalls.
veganForAnimalsPage.financialSupport: A person who eats meat might say they aren't killing anyone. But in reality, this is just an excuse to avoid recognizing the truth. When buying meat at the market or store, a person is actually paying their money for someone else to kill for them. Thus, they participate in animal slaughter through financial support.
veganForAnimalsPage.futureGenerations: We are on this planet for a limited time, and how we interact with it will affect future generations. It is in our interest to minimize the suffering and malpractice suffered by animals and humans, and to move toward a more humane world at peace with itself.
veganForAnimalsPage.humaneWorld: Assuming this is a goal most people desire, we need to improve our treatment of both humans and animals. People generally want to live healthy lives, and the vegetarian path leads in that direction while minimizing the suffering of other animal species.
veganForAnimalsPage.hypocrisy: The entire path of meat consumers is based on hypocrisy. People use some animals as mascots and treat them specially. Other animals are seen as pests or food, so they aren't provided the care and protection that 'mascots' receive.
veganForAnimalsPage.industrialCruelty: Industrial Cruelty: Turning Farms into Factories. The Federal Animal Welfare Act excludes farm animals, and most state laws make exemptions for agricultural practices. These include branding, castration, dehorning, and debeaking, performed without anesthesia.
veganForAnimalsPage.intelligenceArgument: Many animals are less intelligent than humans, but there are considerable variations in intelligence among humans as well. If it's claimed that the low intelligence of many animals gives us the right to treat them as we wish, without real care, then one could also argue that less intelligent people should be treated similarly.
veganForAnimalsPage.pigInMudImg: Pigs enjoy life when given the chance
veganForAnimalsPage.pigsInCages: The movie 'Babe' represents how Americans would like to think about pigs. But 'Babe' piglets don't see the sun during their short lives, nor hay or mud to roll in. Sows live in small cages, so tight they can't even turn around. They spend their lives behind metal bars, with waste falling through into huge pits below.
veganForAnimalsPage.poultrySlaughter: Farm Sanctuary filmed fully conscious chickens having their throats cut and then being stuffed into canisters to bleed. Each year, many chickens, turkeys, ducks, and geese are scalded alive in tanks until they die or drown.
veganForAnimalsPage.slaughterhouseHorrors: According to USDA inspectors, some slaughterhouses skin cattle alive, drown screaming pigs in hot water, and abuse conscious animals in other ways to make them move faster. Federal laws on humane slaughter are increasingly ignored as factory farms grow.
veganForAnimalsPage.transportation: During transport, all farm animals lose at least 3% of their weight due to stress. They are forced to stand in their own waste and are exposed to extreme weather in open trucks.
veganForAnimalsPage.wildlifeProtection: USDA APHIS' Wildlife Service and livestock producers kill wildlife to protect domestic animals. Government hunters kill around 100,000 wild dogs, pigs, bison, and mountain lions each year.

veganForEnvironmentPage.title: Saving the Planet – The Ecological Reason
veganForEnvironmentPage.description: Currently, eating less meat is the most important decision a consumer can make for preserving the environment.
veganForEnvironmentPage.destruction: Raising animals for meat production destroys habitats, causes the extinction of various species, and generates massive water and air pollution.
veganForEnvironmentPage.documentaryHome: All of this can be seen in the documentary 'Home'.
veganForEnvironmentPage.efficiencyComparison: But we all have to eat, right? You will have a clear picture of the inefficiency of meat production if you compare it with fruits, vegetables, and grains. Compared to pasta, red meat consumes 20 times more land, causes 7 times more common pollution, and 5 times more toxic water pollution.
veganForEnvironmentPage.faoReport: The UN FAO reports 21 problems that have been created and substantially worsened due to animal excrement and the increasing need for animal feed:
veganForEnvironmentPage.fossilFuelEnergy: The report also found that fossil fuel energy is a major addition to the industrial production of eggs, milk, and meat, and that factory farms prove inefficient at converting this energy into food for humans.
veganForEnvironmentPage.greenhouseGas: Recent UN research has shown that the livestock industry alone creates more greenhouse gas than transport.
veganForEnvironmentPage.hormonesAndPoisons: Due to hormones, pesticides, and poor-quality feed, animal waste is no longer a fertilizer, but poison. Livestock herds raised for human food produce 130 times more waste than the entire population of the planet!
veganForEnvironmentPage.manureLagoonImgDesc: Waste from an animal farm being dumped into a lagoon (photo courtesy of US EPA)
veganForEnvironmentPage.proteinConversionDesc: Producing one kilogram of meat requires 7 kilograms of grain and 7,000 kilograms of water for grain production. It is neither economic nor logical to feed animals most of the food, especially when half the global population suffers from hunger.
veganForEnvironmentPage.proteinConversionTitle: It takes 16 kilograms of plant protein to produce 1 kilogram of animal protein!
veganForEnvironmentPage.soilErosionImgDesc: Soil erosion along Rio Puerco, New Mexico as a result of overgrazing by animal farms (photo courtesy of US EPA)
veganForEnvironmentPage.airAndWaterPollution: Intensive factory expansion has made the air so unbearable that in some rural communities, residents are forced to wear masks outdoors. Poultry and pig waste have contributed to the increase in pathogenic organisms in waters, poisoning people and killing millions of fish.
veganForEnvironmentPage.toxicBombs: Just think of this comparison: a single farm in Utah produces more waste than the entire city of Los Angeles!!! Animal waste, stored in so-called basins or lagoons (toxic bombs), penetrates the soil, poisons groundwater, and alters the fragile aquatic ecosystem.
veganForEnvironmentPage.unauthorizedGrazing: Unauthorized grazing has caused great environmental damage and land degradation in western North America.
veganForEnvironmentPage.wasteProduction: According to the EPA's 'Animal Waste Management: What's the problem?': 'By definition, AFOs produce large amounts of waste in small spaces. For example, a dairy cow produces about 60 kg of wet manure per day. It is estimated that one cow produces the same amount of waste in one day as humans do in 20-40 days.'
veganForEnvironmentPage.waterPollution: Meat production causes 20% of consumer-polluted water. This industry also uses vast areas of land. Currently, about 20% of the Earth's biomass is made up of animals raised for meat, which is more than the biomass of all humans.

veganForHealthPage.title: Health – The Nutritional Reason
veganForHealthPage.anatomicalStructure: Human anatomical structure – teeth, jaws, and digestive system – are all better adapted for plant-based food. In the 19th century, Carl Linnaeus demonstrated that the native food of humans consists of fruits and vegetables.
veganForHealthPage.chemicalProcessing: Meat is processed with nitrates and carcinogenic substances to prevent decomposition. Large amounts of chemicals are also added to animal feed to increase meat mass.
veganForHealthPage.description: A balanced vegetarian diet is healthier and appropriate for both children and adults.
veganForHealthPage.digestiveSystem: One reason meat harms the body is that the human digestive system is not adapted to process meat alone. Carnivores have short intestines to quickly eliminate toxins. Humans have long intestines, so meat forms toxins that disrupt the body.
veganForHealthPage.diseasePrevention: Frequent meat consumption increases the likelihood of cancer and heart disease. Switching to a vegetarian diet prevents the occurrence and development of heart disease in 90-97% of cases.
veganForHealthPage.healthOpinion: There is a deep-rooted opinion in society that you cannot survive without meat. This opinion is erroneous and not based on solid scientific arguments. On the contrary, recent research has shown that a balanced vegetarian diet is healthier and appropriate for both children and adults.
veganForHealthPage.mortalityCause: After alcoholism and smoking, meat and dairy consumption is the most common cause of mortality in European countries, the USA, and Australia.
veganForHealthPage.plantProteins: Most vegetables, fruits, seeds, nuts, and grains are sources of complete proteins that are more easily assimilated and contain no toxic mixtures. Vegetarians get proteins directly from the source, not 'second hand'.
veganForHealthPage.proteinNorm: For twenty years, the daily protein norm was thought to be 150g, but today it's officially recognized at around 45g. Excessive protein consumption is not only unjustified but harmful to the human body.
veganForHealthPage.workCapacity: Dr. Irving Fisher of Yale University conducted experiments showing that vegetarians have twice the endurance of meat-eaters. Vegetarians are able to work two to three times longer than those who feed on meat.
